item.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
item.description: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
item.description: Introducing GPT-5.5, our smartest model yet—faster, more capable, and built for complex tasks like coding, research, and data analysis across tools.

item.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
item.description: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managing administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data.

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain ...
item.description: Bitwarden CLI 2026.4.0 was compromised in the Checkmarx supply chain campaign after attackers abused a GitHub Action in Bitwarden’s CI/CD pipeline.

item.title: crawshaw - 2026-04-22
Highlights from Git 2.54
item.description: The open source Git project just released Git 2.54. Here is GitHub’s look at some of the most interesting features and changes introduced since last time.

item.title: World Models will push the frontier for LLMs
item.description: Large Language Models are trained using the next-token prediction objective. This means that the vocabulary distribution of the model needs to be close to th...

item.title: Why You Need MLOps: When CI/CD for Machine Learning Becomes Mandatory
item.description: Why MLOps is mandatory: architectural thresholds, Azure vs. GCP cloud patterns, hidden GPU costs, and the real risks of deploying ML without pipelines.
item.author: (Ateljé Vagabond)

item.title: Inside a Massive 1980s Hard Drive: 14 Inches of Fury!
item.description: Dave takes you on a POV tour of his 1979 DEC PDP-11/44, including a look at the CPU complex, inside the massive 14" hard drives, and more!
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Zain Hasan | Inside Claude Code: An Architecture Deep Dive
item.description: A walkthrough of how Claude Code works internally — from its layered architecture and query loop to its tool system and permission model.

item.title: Agents and the Era of Overproduction
You can make something good
item.description: Farza Majeed on bringing your ideas to life

item.title: Can We Measure Software Slop? An Experiment
item.description:
In this article, I propose a definition of software slop based on human attention (slop = code that hasn't been reviewed or verified) and sketch out a way to estimate how "sloppy" a piece of software is.
I put it to the test with Slop-O-Meter, an experimental tool that analyzes public GitHub repos and assigns them a sloppiness score. I then discuss the results of the tool, which are not very reliable, but interesting nonetheless.

item.title: How to Open Source and Not Starve
What an hour of your attention is worth — jonno.nz
item.description: You pay Big Tech about $1,000 a year in attention. Here's how to read the meter — and why building your own is suddenly cheaper than opting out.
item.author: (John Gregoriadis)

item.title: The price of software is going to zero
item.description: The price of software is going to zero. AI assisted programming makes it much faster and cheaper to build software. On the scale of the software market this means that most of what is currently consumer paid software will become free software. Prior to AI only large hyperscalers could afford to offer truly free software. Gmail has been free since its inception. Fastmail charges $60/year for a comparable service. Many SAAS companies have offered ‘free’ tiers over the years. Resend offers 3000 emails a month for free. Mailchimp and ConvertKit offer free tiers for smaller businesses. The goal of these free tiers was to draw in users who might later upgrade to a paid plan. Essentially, the free plan is advertising for the paid plan.

item.title: Don't Read the PDF. Write the Parser. | Adrià Cidre
item.description: I stopped feeding hospital PDFs to a vision model. When the layout changes, the AI fixes the parser instead — and production never sees a token.

item.title: Moving a tenant across PostgreSQL instances · Anantha Kumaran
item.description: In earlier posts, I covered how to move full PostgreSQL data between instances using GCP DMS, and how to move selected tables across instances. In this post, I’ll focus on moving data for a specific tenant from one instance to another.

item.title: Syncing Time Over the Internet
item.description: When you buy a brand new computer—let's say you bought a MacBook—and you open it for the first time, it is not yet connected to any internet. As such, it shows the wrong time. But the moment you connect it to your Wi-Fi for the first time, automatically, it somehow manages to show the correct time. Have you ever wondered how it does this?

item.title: I'm Using Claude Code for Everything Else But Coding
item.description: Nineteen days after cancelling Claude Max, my pattern has settled. Codex with GPT-5.4 on xHigh took the coding seat. Claude Code with Opus 4.7 on xHigh took every other seat at the desk.
item.author: (Chandler Nguyen)

item.title: Most of What We Call Progress | Yusuf Aytas
item.description: What years of software development reveal about progress, process, and people, reflections on why good judgment outlasts tools, and simplicity always wins.

item.title: Writing an LLM from scratch, part 33 -- what I learned from finally getting round to the appendices
item.description: I've finished the main body of Raschka's book -- here are my thoughts on the appendices, and why I'm glad I read them after learning things the hard way.
item.author: (Giles Thomas)

item.title: Coding Models Are Doing Too Much
item.description: Code for this post is available here.

item.title: We Found a Stable Firefox Identifier Linking All Your Private Tor Identities
item.description: We discovered a privacy vulnerability in Firefox Private Browsing and Tor Browser that allows websites to fingerprint and track users across origins using IndexedDB database ordering, even after closing all private windows.

item.title: Show HN submissions tripled and now mostly share the same vibe-coded look
item.description: An attempt to detect AI design patterns in Show HN pages

item.title: Border Message
